id.description: Stable handle for the route used by 'srv route' CLI.

preserve_host.description: Whether to preserve the Host header (default true).

pass_range_headers.description: Forward Range/If-Range headers for byte-range requests.

Route.description:
Route attaches an extra Traefik router to a site, used for path-prefix splits
(e.g. /app → WebSocket on :6001) or regex rewrites (e.g. /videos/...).

Upstream.description:
Upstream points a route at a backend. Exactly one of Port/Container/URL is
set per Kind.

VolumeMount.description:
VolumeMount is an extra bind-mount the user added to a site so its container
can reach host paths beyond the project root (TEMP dirs, nix-profile
binaries, demo asset trees, etc.). Source and Target are absolute paths;
the source must already exist on the host.

domains.description: All hostnames; the first entry is canonical.

project_path.description: Absolute path to the project on disk.

service_name.description: Container name used for Traefik routing.

profile.description: docker-compose profile (if the service uses profiles).

port.description: Port the service listens on inside the container.

is_local.description: Whether to use a locally-issued (mkcert) SSL certificate.

extra_networks.description: Extra external Docker networks the site joins (for reaching user-managed containers like mysql01).

volumes.description: Extra host bind-mounts attached to the site's container (e.g. ~/.nix-profile

listeners.description: Extra Traefik entrypoints (e.g. 'internal' for plain HTTP on :88).

cache.description: Emit aggressive caching headers for static assets.

dockerfile_port.description: Port discovered from the Dockerfile EXPOSE directive.

title: srv site metadata
description:
SiteMetadata holds all configuration for a site.
This is stored in ~/.config/srv/sites/{name}/metadata.yml
